title: 17.-Contract: E.F. Brett & Company, Inc. for MLK Jr. Youth Services Center/Y.A.P Seismic Retrofit and Renovation Project

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution:
1. Approving the plans and specifications for the MLK Jr. Youth Services Center/Y.A.P.
Seismic Retrofit and Renovation project, Specification No. 25-11695-C;
2. Rejecting the bid protest of W.E. Lyons Construction Co., the second lowest bidder;
3. Accepting the bid of the lowest responsive and responsible bidder, E.F. Brett &
Company, Inc.; and
4. Authorizing the City Manager or their designee to execute a contract and any
amendments, extensions or other change orders until completion of the project in
accordance with the approved plans and specifications, with E.F. Brett & Company,
Inc. for the MLK Jr. Youth Services Center/Y.A.P. Seismic Retrofit and Renovation
Project at 1730 Oregon Street, in an amount not to exceed $11,859,100 which
includes a contract amount consisting of the base bid and bid alternates 1, 4 and 5
of $10,781,000 and a 10% contingency in the amount of $1,078,100.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding in the amount of $10,900,000 is available in the FY25 Parks Tax Fund (Fund 138)
and Measure T1 Fund (Fund 511), and an additional Parks Tax amount of $959,100 is
subject to appropriation in the FY 2026 budget.

Contract: E.F. Brett & Company, Inc. for CONSENT CALENDAR
MLK Jr. Youth Services Center/Y.A.P. Seismic Retrofit and Renovation Project June 3, 2025
sections.current_situation:
The existing community center at Martin Luther King Jr. Youth Services Center/Young
Adult Project (Y.A.P.), 1730 Oregon Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 needs renovation.
The center has been identified as a location to be used as a mass care site in the event
of an earthquake or other emergencies per the City of Berkeley’s Local Hazard
Mitigation Plan. This project will provide seismic upgrades to the foundation, roofing,
and wall systems to achieve an “Immediate Occupancy” structural rating to serve the
community in the event of an earthquake or other disaster.
In addition, the building will undergo improvements to circulation, accessibility, utilities,
and finishes to modernize the building to current energy efficiency and building code
standards. New electrical, HVAC, and other infrastructure improvements will allow the
building to act as a community resiliency center in times of need, for events such as
smoke days, as a heating center, or as a cooling center.
Under normal operations, the MLK Jr. Youth Services Center serves the community
with recreational programming and is home to the City’s Young Adult Project (Y.A.P.)
for our most at-risk young adults. Planned improvements will improve circulation, create
new programming spaces, and modernize the facility for improved recreational and
programming opportunities.
sections.background:
The project was advertised for bids on Monday, March 17, 2025, and bids were opened
on April 22, 2025. The City received two bids, from a low base bid of $10,603,000 to a
high base bid of $10,629,126, and from a low of $10,750,987 to a high of $10,781,000
for the base bid plus Additive Bid Alternates No. 1,4, and 5. The determination of the
lowest responsive and responsible bidder was based on the price for base bid work, as
indicated in the bid documents.
On April 28, 2025, the City received a bid protest from W.E. Lyons Construction Co.
(WEL), the second lowest bidder. A copy of the bid protest was sent to E.F. Brett &
Company, Inc. (EFB) to provide them an opportunity to respond to the protest issues.
EFB’s response on April 30, 2025 was then forwarded to WEL for their opportunity to
respond. On May 2, 2025, WEL provided a rebuttal to EFB and on May 5, 2025, EFB
provided a response back to WEL, ensuring that both parties had an opportunity to
present all facts and arguments in support of their positions.
City staff, including staff from the City Attorney’s Office, carefully reviewed and
investigated the protest and responses. Generally, the basis for the protest, among
other things, was that EFB’s bid was non-responsive because EFB (1) failed to submit a
“clearly written” bid, (2) failed to correct contractors’ license numbers and DIR numbers
within 24 hours after bid opening, citing Public Contract Code 4104, and (3) was
ambiguous about which subcontractor EFB intended to use for metal panels work.
Based on the City’s review of the bid protest and responses, the City’s findings are as
follows:

sections.rationale:
The City’s Local Hazard Mitigation Plan identified the MLK Jr. Youth Services Center as
a Care and Shelter Facility for the community and a seismic upgrade to the “Immediate
Occupancy” level will reduce the chance of building collapse in the event of a moderate
or major earthquake. Various upgrades to the building are overdue, including
accessibility upgrades to meet to current building code, and modernization to improve
recreational programming at the site.
sections.alternatives:
None

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: E.F. BRETT & COMPANY, INC. FOR THE
MARTIN LUTHER KING JR. YOUTH SERVICES CENTER/Y.A.P.
SEISMIC RETROFIT AND RENOVATION PROJECT
WHEREAS, the Martin Luther King Jr. Youth Services Center is in need of renovation;
and
WHEREAS, the City has neither the in-house labor nor the equipment necessary to
undertake this project; and
WHEREAS, an invitation for bids was duly advertised on March 17, 2025, bids were
opened on April 22, 2025, and the City received 2 bids; and
WHEREAS, E.F. Brett & Company, Inc. was determined to be the lowest responsive
and responsible bidder, and references for E.F. Brett & Company, Inc. were provided
and checked out satisfactorily; and
WHEREAS, funding in the amount of $10,900,000 is available in the FY25 Parks Tax Fund
(Fund 138) and Measure T1 Fund (Fund 511), and an additional Parks Tax amount of
$959,100 is available, subject to appropriation in the FY 2026 budget.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
Plans and Specifications for Specification No. 25-11695-C for the Martin Luther King Jr.
Youth Services Center/Y.A.P. Seismic Retrofit and Renovation Project are approved;
and
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the bid protest by W.E. Lyons Construction
Company is rejected; and
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the construction contract is awarded to E.F. Brett &
Company, Inc. as the lowest responsive and responsible bidder for a total contract
amount of $11,859,100, which includes the base bid plus bid alternate numbers 1, 4,
and 5; and
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the Council of the City of Berkeley authorizes the
City Manager or their designee to execute a contract and any amendments, extensions,
or change orders until completion of the project in accordance with the approved plans
and specifications with E.F. Brett & Company, Inc. for the Martin Luther King Jr. Youth
Services Center/Y.A.P Seismic Retrofit and Renovation Project in a
